heading: Dispute settlement panels and procedures
label: 3533 Dispute settlement panels and procedures
notes: Effective DateSection effective on the date on which the WTO Agreement enters into force with respect to the United States (), see , set out as a note under .Jan. 1, 1995section 130 of Pub. L. 103–465section 3531 of this title

children.heading: Review by President
children.label: a Review by President
children.text: The President shall review annually the WTO panel roster and shall include the panel roster and the list of persons serving on the Appellate Body in the annual report submitted by the President under .section 2213(a) of this title

children.heading: Qualifications of appointees to panels
children.label: b Qualifications of appointees to panels

children.text: seek to ensure that persons appointed to the WTO panel roster are well-qualified, and that the roster includes persons with expertise in the subject areas covered by the Uruguay Round Agreements; and
inform the President of persons nominated to the roster by other WTO member countries.

children.heading: Rules governing conflicts of interest
children.label: c Rules governing conflicts of interest
children.text: The Trade Representative shall seek the establishment by the General Council and the Dispute Settlement Body of rules governing conflicts of interest by persons serving on panels and members of the Appellate Body and shall describe, in the annual report submitted under , any progress made in establishing such rules.section 3534 of this title

the nature of the dispute, including the matters set forth in the request for the establishment of the panel, the legal basis of the complaint, and the specific measures, in particular any State or Federal law cited in the request for establishment of the panel;
the identity of the persons serving on the panel; and
whether there was any departure from the rule of consensus with respect to the selection of persons to serve on the panel.

children.heading: Notice of appeals of panel reports
children.label: e Notice of appeals of panel reports

children.text: the issues under appeal; and
the identity of the persons serving on the Appellate Body who are reviewing the report of the panel.

children.heading: Actions upon circulation of reports
children.label: f Actions upon circulation of reports

children.text: notify the appropriate congressional committees of the report;
in the case of a report of a panel, consult with the appropriate congressional committees concerning the nature of any appeal that may be taken of the report; and

if the report is adverse to the United States, consult with the appropriate congressional committees concerning whether to implement the report’s recommendation and, if so, the manner of such implementation and the period of time needed for such implementation.

children.heading: Changes in agency regulations or practice
children.label: 1 Changes in agency regulations or practice
A
children.text: the appropriate congressional committees have been consulted under subsection (f);

children.label: B
children.text: the Trade Representative has sought advice regarding the modification from relevant private sector advisory committees established under ;section 2155 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: the head of the relevant department or agency has provided an opportunity for public comment by publishing in the Federal Register the proposed modification and the explanation for the modification;

children.label: D
children.text: the Trade Representative has submitted to the appropriate congressional committees a report describing the proposed modification, the reasons for the modification, and a summary of the advice obtained under subparagraph (B) with respect to the modification;

children.label: E
children.text: the Trade Representative and the head of the relevant department or agency have consulted with the appropriate congressional committees on the proposed contents of the final rule or other modification; and

children.label: F
children.text: the final rule or other modification has been published in the Federal Register.

children.heading: Effective date of modification
children.label: 2 Effective date of modification
children.text: A final rule or other modification to which paragraph (1) applies may not go into effect before the end of the 60-day period beginning on the date on which consultations under paragraph (1)(E) begin, unless the President determines that an earlier effective date is in the national interest.

children.heading: Vote by congressional committees
children.label: 3 Vote by congressional committees
children.text: During the 60-day period described in paragraph (2), the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Finance of the Senate may vote to indicate the agreement or disagreement of the committee with the proposed contents of the final rule or other modification. Any such vote shall not be binding on the department or agency which is implementing the rule or other modification.

children.heading: Inapplicability to ITC
children.label: 4 Inapplicability to ITC
children.text: This subsection does not apply to any regulation or practice of the International Trade Commission.

children.heading: Consultations regarding review of WTO rules and procedures
children.label: h Consultations regarding review of WTO rules and procedures
children.text: Before the review is conducted of the dispute settlement rules and procedures of the WTO that is provided for in the Decision on the Application of the Understanding on Rules and Procedures Governing the Settlement of Disputes, as such decision is set forth in the Ministerial Declarations and Decisions adopted on , together with the Uruguay Round Agreements, the Trade Representative shall consult with the congressional committees regarding the policy of the United States concerning the review.April 15, 1994
